title: HÃ¶egh Evi to Partner With SEMOP Port-La Nouvelle to Develop Strategic Infrastructure for Hydrogen Import to France and Europe
content: HÃ¶egh Evi has signed an MoU with SEMOP Port-La Nouvelle to develop a floating terminal for hydrogen imports at the port, connecting Europe with producers from the Middle East, North Africa, and the Americas. The terminal, expected to import up to 210,000 tonnes of hydrogen annually by 2030, aims to position Port-La Nouvelle as a strategic entry hub for clean energy in Europe. The project will enhance the regionâ€™s energy infrastructure and job growth, supported by partnerships with projects like HySoW Hydrogen and TerÃ©ga. Feasibility assessments and design planning are currently underway.
author: Analysts Account
